findings.title: Real-time Presence Indicators Dependency
findings.description: The implementation of real-time co-editing is dependent on the development of presence indicators that show who is currently viewing or editing a document. This feature is essential to avoid duplicate work and is a prerequisite for enabling simultaneous editing.
assumptions: Presence indicators must be developed before real-time co-editing can be fully implemented.

evidence.excerpt: Requested feature: real-time presence avatars in the document header showing active viewers and editors.

recommendations: Prioritize the development of presence indicators in the MVP.

findings.title: Conflict Resolution Strategy Risk
findings.description: The choice of conflict resolution strategy (Operational Transformation vs. CRDTs) poses a risk as it will significantly impact the user experience and data integrity during simultaneous edits. A decision must be made early to avoid delays in development.
assumptions: The chosen strategy must align with the existing document model and user needs.

evidence.excerpt: As we move toward real-time co-editing, a formal conflict resolution strategy is required.

recommendations: Conduct a thorough evaluation of both strategies and finalize the decision in the MVP phase.

findings.title: Mobile Editing Support Requirement
findings.description: Mobile editing must be prioritized alongside desktop to ensure a consistent user experience. The current locking model on mobile is particularly problematic and should be addressed in the MVP.
assumptions: Mobile users are a significant portion of the user base and require a seamless experience.

evidence.excerpt: As we build real-time collaboration, mobile must be a first-class client from day one.

recommendations: Include mobile editing support in the MVP to avoid alienating mobile users.

findings.title: Document Locking Model Dependency
findings.description: The current document locking model must be replaced with a more flexible approach to support real-time collaboration. This change is necessary to eliminate user frustration and improve the editing experience.
assumptions: The new locking model must integrate seamlessly with the real-time editing features.

evidence.excerpt: CollabDocs currently implements pessimistic locking: when a user opens a document for editing, it is locked to that user.

recommendations: Develop a new document locking model as part of the MVP.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with implementing real-time collaboration features in CollabDocs. Key components such as presence indicators, conflict resolution strategies, and a new document locking model must be prioritized in the MVP phase to ensure a successful launch and maintain competitive positioning.
